critique.verdict: Passable but not yet approval-grade. The decorated hero has a strong monster-arcade hit, and the mobile crop problem is mostly fixed, but the raw black proof still relies too much on repeated athletic slash/chamfer language. The concept is visible, yet the promised per-glyph fang events are uneven and several key glyphs are not distinctive enough at mobile size.
strongestDecisions: Hero treatment is immediately legible and energetic: mint fill, heavy black outline, and purple offset shadow sell the title face well.
Reverse slant, chunky weight, and angular counters create a usable display rhythm without collapsing readability.
Mobile specimen is substantially more functional than the prior brief warns against: the title is complete, centered, and readable.
V, A, F, N, X, 2, 4, 7, and 9 carry enough sharp structural attitude to feel on-brief.
weakestProblems: The raw black alphabet still looks like a generalized esports/arcade italic before it looks like a creature-fang system.

C, G, O, and 0 do not yet prove the recorded construction strongly enough. C and G read as chipped angular letters, O is not carrying a major asymmetric voltage break, and 0 is only moderately distinguished from O.
The repeated small bite cuts are still doing too much of the styling work, especially across E/F/T/A/G/numerals.
Spacing is usable but loose and uneven in proof strings; VOLTAGE FANG has airy gaps while THE QUICK and BROWN TOOL feel more cohesive.
Some internal events get muddy at mobile proof size, especially G, O, 6, 8, ampersand, and punctuation.
visualRisks: Hero decoration flatters the design more than the raw outlines do, which can hide weak glyph construction.
At smaller sizes, many fang details collapse into generic notches rather than memorable silhouette events.
The O/0/6/8/9 family is not differentiated enough to survive real-world title use without confusion or sameness.
The gradient hero background is pleasant but generic; the font itself must carry more identity in black-only contexts.
nextEdits: Proof only the black uppercase and numerals first, without mint, outline, shadow, or gradient.
Redraw C/G/O/0/6/8/9 as a focused group with one large visible body event each, not extra perimeter chips.
Make C an unmistakable open jaw with a lower fang silhouette that changes the aperture shape.
Give G a much larger inward voltage hook that reads before decoration is added.
Make O a closed charged ring with one bold asymmetric break, and make 0 clearly slashed with a different rhythm.
Reduce small repeated chamfer chips by another 30-40% and replace them with fewer, larger structural decisions.
Tighten display spacing for VOLTAGE FANG and CGO AEX while preserving enough room for outlines in the decorated hero.
critique.promptPatch: Next pass: ignore the decorated hero until the raw black proof passes. Keep the reverse-slanted monster-title stance, but remove most repeated esports chamfer chips. Redraw C/G/O/0/6/8/9 with one oversized mobile-visible electric-fang event each: C=open jaw with a lower fang aperture, G=jaw plus large inward voltage hook, O=closed charged ring with one major asymmetric break, 0=obvious slash and rhythm unlike O, 6=open top hook with lower bite, 8=two clearly pinched counters, 9=top loop with descending fang tail. Then retune spacing on VOLTAGE FANG, THE QUICK, BROWN TOOL, CGO AEX, and 0123456789 before applying mint fill, black sticker outline, and purple shadow.
critique.referenceStrategy: Use the desktop proof for spacing and silhouette diagnosis, then judge the same words on mobile before adding color. Compare C/G/O/0/6/8/9 as a separate black-only control strip at the smallest specimen size.
critique.memoryLesson: Do not approve Voltage Fang just because the hero looks exciting. Approval requires the undecorated black proof to show large, individualized electric-fang construction, especially in C/G/O and 0/6/8/9, with spacing tuned at mobile size.
